name: The First National Bank Of Frederick
description_short: Locally owned community bank in Frederick, South Dakota, offering checking, savings, CDs, and digital banking since 1882 with a focus on personal service and agricultural lending.
description_long:
First National Bank is a five-generation, locally owned community bank headquartered in Frederick, South Dakota, with a 140+ year history of serving the region since 1882. The bank emphasizes its roots in the community and its commitment to personal banking relationships, contrasting itself with larger national banks by making lending decisions locally rather than at distant corporate headquarters.

The bank offers a full range of retail banking services including checking accounts, savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), and debit cards. They provide both consumer and business online and mobile banking platforms, allowing customers 24/7 access to their accounts from smartphones and computers. The bank is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, meeting all federal banking standards and protections.

First National Bank distinguishes itself through its explicit focus on agricultural lending and support for agricultural producers in the region. The bank markets itself on providing personal service, relationship banking, and local decision-making—features it argues are unavailable from larger banking institutions. Their emphasis on community involvement is reflected in their publishing of the Frederick Area F.Y.I. newsletter serving multiple communities in northern Brown County.

The bank appears to be a traditional community bank with standard retail banking services. While the website emphasizes personal service and local decision-making advantages, it provides limited information about specific rates, fees, loan products, or competitive terms. Prospective customers would need to contact the bank directly for pricing, APR details, and specific product terms. The focus on agriculture suggests stronger lending capabilities in that sector, but details on business loan programs beyond online banking access are not specified on the website. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Checking accounts with multiple account options
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) for fixed-term investing
Debit cards with in-person, phone, and online payment capability
Consumer online banking with 24/7 account access
Business online banking for account management
Digital banking account access and management
pros: Five-generation locally owned bank with 140+ years of history in the community (since 1882)
FDIC-insured with full federal backing and deposit protection
Locally-made lending decisions designed specifically to help agricultural producers succeed
Offers both consumer and business online and mobile banking with 24/7 access
Emphasizes personal service and relationship banking as alternative to large national banks
Provides full suite of basic banking products including checking, savings, CDs, and debit cards
Serves multiple communities in northern Brown County through active community engagement
cons: Limited geographic availability as a single-location Frederick, South Dakota community bank
Website provides no specific information on interest rates, APYs, fees, or account minimums
No details disclosed about specific business loan products, lines of credit, or SBA lending programs
Limited information about mortgage products, personal loans, or credit card offerings
Smaller institution may have fewer branch locations and ATM networks compared to regional/national banks
best_for: Agricultural producers and farmers in northern Brown County, South Dakota seeking local relationship lending
Community residents of Frederick and surrounding areas (Barnard, Westport, Elm Lake) who value personal banking relationships
Small business owners in the region seeking locally-made lending decisions
Customers prioritizing personalized service and community connection over national bank convenience
diagnosis: First National Bank is best suited for Frederick-area residents and regional agricultural producers who prioritize personal relationships, local decision-making, and community connection in their banking. The main caveat is that as a single-location community bank, it offers limited geographic convenience, fewer ATM/branch locations, and minimal publicly available information about rates and fees compared to regional or national banks—requiring direct contact for specific product terms.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
